Yeah NDX sounds way better for this kind of stuff. I always wondered why people stick with QQQ when you have these weird expiration rules that can mess up your timing. RH really loves to screw people over with their random restrictions - like they're protecting us from making money or something

  
The UI is pretty but not worth losing trades like that, especially when market is moving fast at end of day
First close below 50WMA since April low, soft support all the way down to $640 on QQQ. Grats bears, looks like this is it.

We're gyrating around and going through some motions but this is a textbook price correction after an insane-o run up in a narrow theme.

QQQ (and the Nasdaq is driving this) has to go down to about 670 to fill a couple gaps which is coincidentally about a 10% drop from the top and also connects with the first major moving averages on a 1 day interval chart.

Y'all can say this is hocus pocus technical bullshit but odds are it plays out something like this.  

There is no major change in the market outlook or key themes just a need to pull back on prices between earnings seasons.
If you liked this almost 10% pullback on QQQ you’ll love it by August when it’s down 70% more.
range bound to the cents now because QQQ algos don't want it to drop sub 696.

Been following some of these defense names for while now and this data is pretty solid. LDOS especially caught my attention since those congressional selling patterns stopping is usually good indicator something brewing. The timing with all these contracts dropping June 8th definitely not coincidence.

  
One thing I learned from tracking defense stocks is that mid cap ones like these tend to have more volatile moves when contracts hit compared to the big boys. Your backtesting confirms what I've seen - LMT and RTX barely move anymore on contract news since everyone expects them to win everything anyway. But these smaller players can really pop when they land something significant.

  
PSN looks interesting even without the political buying signals - that T+90 performance in your data is pretty compelling. BWXT worries me bit though with that low hit rate on longer timeframe, might be more of quick swing play. Defense sector overall has been getting lot of tailwinds lately so timing seems right for this kind of strategy.
